boundary_binding.action_boundary: May AP release the invoice while the ERP hold remains visible?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: Policy STD-RAV-072-P requires closeout ledger to match invoice, vendor, amount, tax review, quality signoff, AND payment release approval before payment. CLOSE-RAV-072 confirms only the first five items; payment release approval remains open, violating the exact policy literalism required.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: CLOSE-RAV-072 explicitly states 'payment release approval remains open,' directly contradicting the policy's requirement for payment release approval before payment. The visible ERP hold HOLD-RAV-072 corroborates the open state with 'payment-release queue' listed as a remaining reason.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: The 'payment-release queue' dependency identified in HOLD-RAV-072 and confirmed open in CLOSE-RAV-072 remains unresolved, blocking invoice release under exact policy interpretation. No substitute or workaround is permitted under source-boundary verification rules.

critical_features_preserved: payment release approval dependency not bypassed
final_answer: ESCALATE. CLOSE-RAV-072 confirms that payment release approval remains open, directly violating policy STD-RAV-072-P which requires closure before release. HOLD-RAV-072 remains visible with payment-release queue as a listed reason. The source boundary is not closed; the invoice cannot be released despite other items being matched. Escalation is required to resolve the open payment approval dependency.
open_blockers: payment release approval not closed per CLOSE-RAV-072
ERP hold reason includes payment-release queue per HOLD-RAV-072
policy literalism requires exact payment release approval closure before release
